figures.caption: Figure 1. Illustration of different spatiotemporal memory update paradigms for echocardiography video segmentation. Top: Memory bank methods rely on sparse key-frame retrieval to capture temporal dynamics. Middle: Linear recurrent models perform unconstrained element-wise updates for state propagation. Bottom: Our OSA enforces a Stiefel manifold constraint, yielding stable and drift-free tracking across the cardiac cycle.

Table 1. Comparison of OSA with state-of-the-art methods for LV segmentation on the CAMUS [25] and EchoNet-Dynamic [34] datasets. Metrics include mean Dice coefficient (mDice) and mean 95% Hausdorff distance (mHD95) for segmentation evaluation, as well as correlation coefficient (corr) and bias±std (%) for LVEF estimation.

Table 2. Comparison of different models on CAMUS. Paradigm denotes the core temporal memory and update mechanism; Para is the number of parameters (M); Mem is the GPU memory usage during training (GB); and Time is the approximate training time (hours) to reach the best mDice.
Figure 8. Heatmap ablation on EchoNet-Dynamic. Warmer colors indicate higher prediction confidence.

Figure 7. Visual comparison with state-of-the-art methods. The top two rows are from CAMUS, and the bottom two rows are from EchoNet-Dynamic. Green, red, and yellow regions represent the ground truth, prediction, and overlapping regions, respectively.

Figure A.2. Spatiotemporal training dynamics visualization. Qualitative results on CAMUS patient 0225 (4-chamber view) at four training stages. For each stage (a-d), the top row displays input frames with segmentation masks, and the bottom row shows the corresponding feature activation maps. The progression from (a) to (d) illustrates the transition from diffused spatial activations and coarse masks to localized feature representations and temporally consistent segmentations throughout the cardiac cycle.

Figure 3. Overview of the OSA architecture. The Anatomical Prior-aware Feature Enhancement (APFE) encoder extracts contrastdecomposed features from echocardiography video frames, which are recurrently updated through the Orthogonalized State Update (OSU) mechanism to maintain stable and geometry-aware spatiotemporal representations. The decoder reconstructs segmentation maps, and the entire model is optimized with loss.
Figure A.1. 3D geometric intuition of update directions on the loss
